question: You can use global examples for this question. Each of the examples should however cite a different brand. i) In the context of ingredient co-branding give an example for new attribute expansion ii) In the context of co-branding give an example of modifier and header iii) Give one example for brand communication where celebrities appeared to distract the viewer from the message. 100 words, 10 marks

answers.brand: Gore-Tex + North Face / Doritos + Taco Bell / Celine Dion + Chrysler
answers.brand_concept: Gore-Tex adds waterproof-breathable attribute = ingredient new attribute; Doritos modifies Taco Bell = modifier-header; Celine Dion overshadowed Chrysler = vampire effect
answers.framework: Ingredient co-branding (Keller Ch.8) + Modifier-Header + Vampire Effect
answers.answer_text:
i) Ingredient co-branding creates new attributes via a branded component from a different company. Gore-Tex (W.L. Gore) partners with The North Face to add waterproof-breathable protection—an attribute the apparel brand lacked alone. "Gore-Tex membranes block wind and water droplets while allowing vapor escape" [1]. This is new attribute expansion: the ingredient adds a previously absent functional benefit.

ii) In co-branding, the header is the primary brand; the modifier qualifies it. In Doritos Locos Tacos, Taco Bell is the header (remains a taco), Doritos is the modifier changing only the shell. CEO Greg Creed: "to be a better Taco Bell, the obvious solution was the Doritos Locos Tacos" [2].

iii) The vampire effect: celebrity overshadows brand message. Chrysler signed Celine Dion for $14M to promote the Pacifica, but "the commercials showed Ms. Dion singing and driving. Consumers weren't motivated to visit showrooms" [3]. Dealers sold only 4,828 units in three months vs. 60,000 annual target.

framework_source.cite_as: Keller, K.L., Strategic Brand Management, Ch. 8

citations.author: Gore-Tex Brand
citations.title: What is GORE-TEX Technology
citations.quoted_text: GORE-TEX membranes are constructed using an extremely thin, microporous design with pores small enough to block wind and water droplets from entering, but large enough to allow water-vapor escape.

citations.author: Ben Popken
citations.title: Taco Bell cool ranch tacos: co-branding genius
citations.quoted_text: To be a better Taco Bell, the obvious solution was the Doritos Locos Tacos.

citations.author: Jason Stein
citations.title: Inside Chrysler Celine Dion Advertising Disaster
citations.quoted_text: The lush black-and-white Pacifica commercials showed Ms. Dion singing and driving. But consumers were not motivated to visit the showrooms. Dealers sold only 4,828 Pacificas in the first three months.

answers.brand: Corning Gorilla Glass + OnePlus / GoPro + Red Bull / Gronkowski + FanDuel
answers.brand_concept: Gorilla Glass adds scratch-resistance = ingredient; GoPro modifies Red Bull events = modifier-header; Gronkowski stunt overshadowed FanDuel = vampire effect
answers.answer_text:
i) Ingredient co-branding adds a new attribute via another firm's branded component. Corning's Gorilla Glass is embedded in OnePlus smartphones, adding scratch-resistance and drop-durability the phone brand cannot claim alone. "Gorilla Glass has become the go-to choice for durable, scratch-resistant screens" with over 8 billion devices equipped [1].

ii) Header = primary brand; modifier = secondary qualifier. In the GoPro–Red Bull partnership, Red Bull is the header (events and content remain Red Bull's), GoPro is the modifier adding immersive POV technology. "GoPro is now Red Bull's exclusive provider of point-of-view imaging technology" [2].

iii) FanDuel's 2023 Super Bowl campaign featured Rob Gronkowski attempting a live field goal. Viewers recalled the celebrity kick stunt, not the betting platform. One observer admitted forgetting it was a FanDuel campaign entirely, calling it "anecdotal evidence of the vampire effect in action" [3].

framework_source.cite_as: Keller, K.L., Strategic Brand Management, Ch. 8

citations.author: David Strand
citations.title: Ingredient Branding in B2B Tech: Lessons from Intel Inside
citations.quoted_text: Corning's Gorilla Glass has become the go-to choice for durable, scratch-resistant screens used in smartphones, tablets, and laptops.

citations.author: Rebecca Stewart
citations.title: Red Bull and GoPro ink exclusive global partnership
citations.quoted_text: GoPro will now be Red Bull's exclusive provider of point-of-view imaging technology.

citations.author: GS Branding
citations.title: Beware the Vampire Effect in Advertising
citations.quoted_text: Anecdotal evidence of the vampire effect in action -- the author admits forgetting that a 2023 pre-Super Bowl ad featuring Rob Gronkowski kicking a field goal was a FanDuel campaign.

answers.brand: Qualcomm Snapdragon + phones / Fiat 500 by Diesel / Pierce Brosnan + Pan Bahar
answers.brand_concept: Snapdragon adds AI-processing = ingredient; Diesel modifies Fiat 500 = modifier-header; Brosnan Bond persona overshadowed Pan Bahar = vampire effect
answers.answer_text:
i) Ingredient co-branding expands attributes via another company's branded component. Qualcomm's Snapdragon chipset adds premium AI-processing and 5G capability the phone maker cannot produce alone. "Consumers are three times more willing to pay for a Snapdragon SoC and do not mind forking over a 16 percent premium" [1].

ii) Header = primary product; modifier = secondary association. The Fiat 500 by Diesel is a co-branded limited edition: Fiat is the header (the car), Diesel (fashion label) is the modifier adding denim upholstery and urban styling. "A collaboration between Fiat's Style Centre and fashion label DIESEL's design team" [2].

iii) Pierce Brosnan endorsed Pan Bahar pan masala in India (2016), but his James Bond persona overshadowed the product entirely. "The last thing one can imagine 007 James Bond doing is chewing pan masala" [3]. Social media ridiculed Brosnan, not Pan Bahar—viewers remembered Bond, forgot the brand.

framework_source.section: Ingredient branding, Modifier-Header, Celebrity vampire effect
framework_source.cite_as: Keller, K.L., Strategic Brand Management, Ch. 8

citations.author: Omar Sohail
citations.title: Qualcomm Survey Reveals Consumers Three Times More Willing To Purchase Snapdragon Smartphones
citations.quoted_text: Respondents are three times more willing to pay for a Snapdragon SoC and do not mind forking over a 16 percent premium.

citations.author: Fiat Group Automobiles UK Ltd
citations.title: 500byDIESEL Now Available Exclusively On-line
citations.quoted_text: The 500byDIESEL, a global limited edition run of just 10,000 examples, is the result of a collaboration between Fiat Style Centre in Turin and fashion label DIESEL design team.

citations.author: Dileep Thekkethil
citations.title: Former James Bond Pierce Brosnan acts in Indian pan masala ad, gets ridiculed
citations.quoted_text: The last thing one can imagine 007 James Bond doing is chewing pan masala before he beats criminals black and blue.

answers.brand: Dolby Atmos + AMC / Doritos + Taco Bell (sales data) / Aishwarya+SRK+Hrithik watches
answers.brand_concept: Dolby Atmos adds immersive 3D audio = ingredient; Doritos modifier on Taco Bell with $1B sales; Multiple stars endorsing competing watches = celebrity clutter
answers.framework: Ingredient co-branding + Modifier-Header + Vampire Effect / Celebrity confusion
answers.answer_text:
i) Dolby Atmos (Dolby Laboratories) is integrated into AMC Theatres as Dolby Cinema at AMC, adding immersive 3D spatial audio—an attribute standard screens lack. "By featuring the Dolby Atmos logo, manufacturers attract audiophiles who associate the technology with a high-quality, immersive experience" [1].

ii) In Doritos Locos Tacos, Taco Bell is the header (product sold at Taco Bell as a taco), Doritos is the modifier (changes only the shell flavour). The product "surpassed 100 million units in 10 weeks" and crossed $1 billion in total sales [2].

iii) "The star presence is all pervasive; the brand becomes a runner up while the celebrity hogs the limelight" [3]. Kamini Banga notes Aishwarya Rai, Shah Rukh Khan, and Hrithik Roshan all endorse different foreign watch brands simultaneously—making it impossible for consumers to recall which star sells which watch. Celebrity clutter destroys brand recall.

framework_source.section: Ingredient branding, Modifier-Header, Celebrity vampire effect
framework_source.cite_as: Keller, K.L., Strategic Brand Management, Ch. 8; Banga, K., 2017

citations.author: David Strand
citations.title: Ingredient Branding in B2B Tech: Lessons from Intel Inside
citations.quoted_text: By featuring the Dolby Atmos logo, these manufacturers attract audiophiles who associate the technology with a high-quality, immersive experience.

citations.author: Team TBH
citations.title: A Case Study of Doritos Locos Tacos Campaign
citations.quoted_text: Surpassing 100 million DLTs sold within the first 10 weeks, reaching over a billion DLTs sold to date.

citations.author: Kamini Banga
citations.title: Celebrities and the Vampire Effect
citations.quoted_text: The star presence and charisma is all pervasive. The brand becomes a runner up while the celebrity hogs the limelight.

chatgpt.response: i) Nike ACG jackets using Gore-Tex fabric illustrate ingredient co-branding that expands product attributes. ii) Courtyard by Marriott is a classic header-modifier structure. iii) Pepsi 2017 Kendall Jenner protest-themed ad drew criticism because Jenner celebrity presence overshadowed the unity message.
